task: Train a linear regression model on synthetic data using scikit-learn. Generate 1000 samples with y = 3*x1 + 0.5*x2 - 2*x3 + noise. Split 80/20 train/test. Compute and print MSE, MAE, R2. Plot actual vs predicted values. Also train with polynomial features (degree 2) and compare. Save the model with joblib and load it back for inference.
description: Train linear regression on synthetic data with comparison
expected_output: A linear regression model with evaluation and persistence

task: Build a spam classifier using scikit-learn. Load the SMS Spam Collection dataset (download from URL). Preprocess: lowercase, remove punctuation, tokenize, remove stopwords, stem. Extract features with TF-IDF. Train a Naive Bayes classifier. Evaluate with precision, recall, F1, confusion matrix. Try Logistic Regression too and compare. Print classification report.
description: Build a spam classifier with TF-IDF and Naive Bayes
expected_output: A spam classifier with preprocessing and comparison

task: Train a neural network on the MNIST dataset using PyTorch. Build a simple CNN with 2 convolutional layers + 2 fully connected layers. Use DataLoader with batching. Train for 5 epochs, track training/validation loss. Plot loss curves. Evaluate on test set and print accuracy. Save and load model weights. Make predictions on 5 random test images.
description: Train a CNN on MNIST with PyTorch
expected_output: A trained CNN with loss curves and test evaluation
eval_criteria: CNN architecture with Conv + FC layers

task: Implement K-Means clustering from scratch in Python (no sklearn). Initialize centroids with k-means++. Run for max 100 iterations or until convergence. Support multiple distance metrics (euclidean, manhattan, cosine). Compute inertia (within-cluster sum of squares). Find optimal k using elbow method. Visualize clusters with matplotlib for 2D data.
description: Implement K-Means from scratch with k-means++
expected_output: A from-scratch K-Means implementation with elbow method

task: Create a train/val/test split utility in Python. Support: random split, stratified split (by labels), time-based split (by date column), and group split (ensure groups don't leak across sets). Configurable ratios (default 70/15/15). Reproducible with random_state. Handle edge cases: small datasets, imbalanced classes. Add type hints and docstrings.
description: Build a robust train/val/test split utility
expected_output: A reusable data splitting utility with multiple strategies

task: Build a feature scaler class in Python (similar to sklearn's StandardScaler and MinMaxScaler). Implement fit(), transform(), fit_transform(), and inverse_transform(). Support both StandardScaler (z-score) and MinMaxScaler (range [0,1]). Store fitted parameters. Handle edge cases: constant features (std=0), single sample. Test on sample data.
expected_output: A feature scaler class with fit/transform/inverse

task: Train a Decision Tree classifier on the Iris dataset using scikit-learn. Tune hyperparameters (max_depth, min_samples_split, min_samples_leaf) with GridSearchCV and 5-fold cross-validation. Print best parameters and CV score. Visualize the tree with plot_tree. Compute feature importance. Evaluate on held-out test set with confusion matrix and classification report.
description: Train and tune a Decision Tree on Iris with GridSearchCV
expected_output: A tuned Decision Tree with visualization and evaluation

task: Create a model evaluation metrics module in Python. Implement functions for: accuracy, precision, recall, f1_score, confusion_matrix, mae, mse, rmse, r2_score, and mean_absolute_percentage_error. Support both binary and multi-class classification. Add macro/micro/weighted averaging options. Write unit tests for each function.
description: Build a model evaluation metrics module
expected_output: A metrics module with classification and regression metrics

task: Create a data augmentation pipeline for images using Python. Implement: random rotation (±30°), random horizontal/vertical flip, random brightness/contrast adjustment, random crop, and Gaussian noise. Use PIL/Pillow (no torchvision). Support chaining with configurable probability per augmentation. Visualize original vs augmented images in a 2xN grid using matplotlib.
description: Build an image augmentation pipeline with Pillow
expected_output: An image augmentation pipeline with configurable transforms

task: Train a simple RNN for text generation using PyTorch. Use a small text corpus (e.g., Shakespeare sonnet or any public domain text). Build a character-level RNN with embedding + LSTM + linear layers. Train to predict next character. Generate 200 characters of text with temperature sampling (support temperature parameter 0.5-2.0). Plot training loss over epochs.
description: Train a character-level RNN for text generation
expected_output: A character-level RNN that generates text with temperature sampling

task: Build a hyperparameter grid search utility (like GridSearchCV). Implement: GridSearch class with fit(X, y) method that tries all parameter combinations. Support parameter grid as dict of {param_name: [values]}. Use k-fold cross-validation (configurable k, default 5). Track best_params_ and best_score_. Support parallel execution with concurrent.futures. Compare with sklearn's GridSearchCV.
description: Build a grid search cross-validation utility
expected_output: A from-scratch GridSearchCV with parallel support

task: Train a logistic regression model on the Titanic dataset. Load data from a URL. Handle missing values: age (median), embarked (mode), cabin (drop). Create features: family_size, title (from name), is_alone. Encode categorical variables. Scale numerical features. Train logistic regression. Evaluate with accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC, and confusion matrix. Plot ROC curve.
description: Train a logistic regression on Titanic with feature engineering
expected_output: A logistic regression model with feature engineering and ROC curve

task: Create a confusion matrix visualization utility in Python. Accept y_true and y_pred (or a pre-computed matrix). Plot a heatmap with: actual values in cells, colorbar, axis labels (Predicted / Actual), tick labels for class names, and value annotations. Support normalization (rows, columns, or overall). Use matplotlib/seaborn. Handle imbalanced classes gracefully.
description: Build a confusion matrix visualizer with heatmap
expected_output: A reusable confusion matrix plotting function

task: Create a learning rate scheduler utility in PyTorch. Implement: StepLR (decay by factor every N steps), CosineAnnealingLR, ExponentialLR, ReduceLROnPlateau (reduce when metric plateaus), and OneCycleLR. Support composable schedulers (chain multiple). Use a callback-style API. Visualize learning rate over epochs for each scheduler type.
description: Build a learning rate scheduler utility for PyTorch
expected_output: A learning rate scheduler with multiple strategies and visualization

task: Train a Random Forest classifier on the UCI Wine Quality dataset. Load from URL. Perform EDA: distribution of quality scores, correlation heatmap, feature pairplots. Handle class imbalance (quality scores 3-8, group into 3 classes: low/medium/high). Train Random Forest with 100 trees. Feature importance plot. Compare with XGBoost (install xgboost). Print both classification reports.
description: Train Random Forest on Wine Quality with EDA and XGBoost comparison
expected_output: A Random Forest model with EDA and XGBoost comparison
eval_criteria: EDA with correlation heatmap and pairplots
